attributes.@qa08: AUDIT COMPLETE 2026-04-09. CORRECTNESS: 5/5 PASS — all stored answers verified independently (Q1:8, Q2:17, Q3:54, Q4:12, Q5:8), integer arithmetic clean, no float risk. SKILL ALIGNMENT WARN: Q4 (x/1=12) and Q5 (x+0=8) are degenerate — divisor=1 and addend=0 both make x=b by inspection with no inverse operation required. Students see the answer without solving. RANDOMIZATION WARN: gen() draws a from 1..20 (addition) and 1..12 (division) with no lower-bound guard. a=0 in addition and a=1 in division both reach the degenerate state. Distinct output space is large (~744 combos) so collision risk is LOW overall. HINT QUALITY WARN: hint2 pattern for Q1–Q3 completes the arithmetic for the student ('Subtract 7 from both sides: 15−7', 'Multiply both sides by 6: 9×6'). hint2 should set up the operation but stop before computing the answer. hint1 labels are correct and appropriate. AGE BAND: PASS — number ranges (a up to 20, b up to 50) appropriate for Gr 6–8 when degenerate cases are excluded.
attributes.@qa09: FIXES APPLIED 2026-04-09 · File: app/public/echo-heist/data.js · FIX 1 (makeT1 hint2): changed hint2 from template literal '${q.n} × ${q.p/100}' (which printed the full multiplication e.g. '80 × 0.25') to 'Multiply ${q.n} by ${q.p/100}' — now names the operation without completing it. FIX 2 (makeT12 hint2): replaced all 6 hint2 strings that showed computed arithmetic results (e.g. '40 − 15', '24 ÷ 3', '18 ÷ 2') with question-form prompts ('What is 40 minus 15?', 'What is 24 divided by 3?') — student must now perform the step. Note: no generator a-range guard needed — makeT1 uses a static 10-entry authored bank with no degenerate cases; makeT12 algebra questions also static, all well-formed. No further fixes outstanding for T1.

ops.insert: ✅ COMPLETE 2026-04-09 — procedural generator + 10 Echo Heist narrative wrappers + clean hints

attributes.@qa08: COMPLETE 2026-04-09. File: app/public/echo-heist/data.js. Three-phase rewrite: (1) Static 5-item percent bank replaced with 10 verified two-step algebra equations. (2) Static bank replaced with deterministic seeded procedural generator — a∈{2,3,4,5,6}, x∈{3..12}, b∈{2..15}, 700 distinct triples, no Math.random(). (3) Narrative wrappers added: 5 Form-A scenarios (hacker billing, electrical grid, courier logistics, timing/cycles, agent tokens) and 5 Form-B scenarios (heat signature, data throughput, runner distance, vault lock, drone signal). Wrapper index = floor(seed/2)%5, so adjacent mission seeds (0/1, 2/3, …) share a scenario but flip A↔B — maximum variety across consecutive missions. All invariants preserved: same seed always returns same question; Form B safety clamp (c≥2); c≤87 across full parameter space; hint1 names both inverse operations; hint2 is question-prompt only naming the division step without revealing x. 10-seed trace verified: seeds 0–9 all produce correct integer answers with contextually coherent narrative text.
attributes.@qa09: No fixes outstanding. T2 is fully production-ready. Optional future enhancements (none blocking): (A) Add 5 more scenario themes (e.g. biometric scan, energy cell, guard patrol, data shard, elevator shaft) to reach wIdx%10 for even richer variety. (B) Parameterise unit labels per wrapper so hint1/hint2 can reference the domain unit name ('files', 'relays', 'floors', etc.) for extra pedagogical clarity — currently hint1/hint2 are unit-agnostic, which is correct but slightly generic.

ops.insert: ✅ FIX DOCUMENTED — toFixed(4) on float calculation

attributes.@qa08: AUDIT COMPLETE 2026-04-07. SHA: 3b673e601fe372f1ee136a3bc3a17e9258fc3e70. All authored D3 answers verified correct (integers throughout). Generator risk: Camera Commons and Escape Lines use raw float arithmetic `a*(1-b/100)` with no toFixed(). In practice the 0.01 tolerance in checkAnswer absorbs the drift numerically, BUT the answer string stored in the prompt object can contain artifacts like '51.00000000000001'. This artifact surfaces in two places: (1) the H-key hint shows the raw answer value in some hint2 strings; (2) any future logging or display of prompt.answer would show broken floats. D3 M26p4 answer '7.2%' correctly normalised. D3 M28p3 (96 * 0.875 = 84) clean. All D3 authored multiplications produce clean integers.
attributes.@qa09: FIX (LOW): In Camera Commons and Escape Lines T3 generator mk() functions, wrap the answer calculation: answer: '' + parseFloat((a*(1-b/100)).toFixed(4)) — this eliminates float artifact strings while preserving precision. Similarly fix the increase variant: parseFloat((a*(1+b/100)).toFixed(4)). This is a one-line fix per generator branch. No authored D3 questions require changes.

attributes.@qa08: AUDIT COMPLETE 2026-04-07. SHA: 3b673e601fe372f1ee136a3bc3a17e9258fc3e70. Generator analysis: Camera Commons T4 generator produces decimal values '0.5','0.10'...'0.45' and percent values '10%','20%'...'90%'. Decimal→Percent: parseFloat(a)*100 produces clean integers for all possible inputs (0.05..0.45 * 100 = 5..45). Percent→Decimal: parseFloat(a.replace('%',''))/100 produces clean decimals (0.1..0.9). normalizeAnswer correctly strips trailing % before comparison. D3 authored questions verified: M21p3 (0.045→4.5%) ✓, M22p4 (18%→0.18) ✓, M27p4 (0.72→72%) ✓, M28v3 (0.125→12.5%) normalisation confirmed correct. Equivalent-form confusion is by design (tests one direction). No issues found.
attributes.@qa09: No fix required. Generator and normaliser are sound for all T4 cases. Optional enhancement: document in code comments that percent-decimal equivalence is intentionally one-directional.

ops.insert: T5 — Fraction of Quantity (a/b of n)
✅ FIX DOCUMENTED — trivial answer guard + Math.round() for both banks

attributes.@qa08: AUDIT COMPLETE 2026-04-07. SHA: 3b673e601fe372f1ee136a3bc3a17e9258fc3e70. TWO ISSUES: (1) TRIVIAL ANSWER: Training Gallery generator uses pairs [[1,2],[1,3],[2,3],[1,4],[3,4],[2,5],[3,5]] with c = b*(1+rand*6). Minimum c = b*1, so min answer for [1,2] is (1/2)*2=1, for [1,3] is (1/3)*3=1. Answer=1 is trivially obvious to students. No minimum-answer guard exists in the code. (2) FLOAT ARTIFACTS IN HINTS: Escape Lines generator uses `answer: '' + (a/b)*c` with pairs [[3,8],[5,6],[7,10],[2,7],[5,8]]. (5/6)*6 = 5.000000000000001 in JS. The H-key hint displays prompt.answer directly, so students see broken float strings. D3 authored T5 questions (M24p1=36, M25e1=51, M30p1=84) are all clean integers.
attributes.@qa09: FIX 1 (MEDIUM — trivial answers): In Training Gallery T5 generator, add minimum answer guard: do { [a,b] = pairs[rand]; c = b*(1+Math.floor(rand*6)); } while ((a/b)*c < 3); This prevents answer=1 or 2. FIX 2 (LOW — float artifacts): In both Training Gallery and Escape Lines T5 mk(), change answer: '' + (a/b)*c to answer: '' + Math.round((a/b)*c). All valid T5 prompts guarantee integer answers (c is always a multiple of b), so Math.round() is safe and eliminates the float artifact without changing any correct answer.

ops.insert: ✅ FIX DOCUMENTED — roundTo() helper + Training Gallery + Escape Lines generators patched

attributes.@qa08: AUDIT COMPLETE 2026-04-07. SHA: 3b673e601fe372f1ee136a3bc3a17e9258fc3e70. SYSTEMATIC ERROR CONFIRMED. Training Gallery T10 generator: mk() computes `parseFloat((Math.round(a/b)*b).toFixed(1))` where b=0.1. The division a/b introduces float drift BEFORE rounding: 6.65/0.1 = 66.49999999999999 → Math.round = 66 → 66*0.1 = 6.6 → answer '6.6'. Correct answer is 6.7. Similarly 2.45/0.1 = 24.499999999999996 → rounds to 24 → answer '2.4'. Correct is 2.5. These are systematically wrong: students who know rounding correctly will be marked incorrect. Escape Lines T10 generator has the same pattern for b=0.01: 3.845/0.01 = 384.49999999999994 → rounds to 384 → answer '3.84'. Correct is 3.85. D3 authored questions are unaffected (use safe integer paths or values that avoid the .x5 boundary exactly).
attributes.@qa09: PATCH READY — see app/docs/echo-heist-patch-2026-04-07.md FIX 1-3. Apply roundTo() helper + update both T10 mk()/gen() calls. Verification: roundTo(6.65,1)===6.7, roundTo(2.45,1)===2.5, roundTo(3.845,2)===3.85.

ops.insert: Affected generators: Training Gallery bank (b=0.1) and Escape Lines bank (b=0.01)
Both generators compute the rounded answer as:

The division a/b is done in float before Math.round(), and JS float representation of decimal fractions is not exact. This causes the value passed to Math.round() to land just below the .5 boundary, flipping it down instead of up.
Concrete proof cases (paste in browser console to verify):
Use exponential notation to shift the decimal point into integer space before rounding, then shift back. This avoids the float division entirely.
Add this helper once, near the top of the <script> block (after the CONSTANTS section, before DISTRICT CONFIG):
function roundTo(val, decimals) { return Number(Math.round(+(val + 'e' + decimals)) + 'e-' + decimals); }
roundTo(7.445, 2)  →  7.45 ✓  (D3 M29v2 authored case still passes)
Diff 1 — Training Gallery bank, T10 mk() function
Location: inside banks['Training Gallery'] array, 4th entry (the Round {a} to nearest {b} template). Search for the string: Math.round(a/b)*b).toFixed(1)

gen(): change (Math.random()*10).toFixed(2) to (Math.random()*10).toFixed(1)  — this restricts generated values to 1dp (e.g. 6.6, 3.4) rather than 2dp (e.g. 6.65, 3.47), eliminating the .x5 boundary class as a secondary guard. Students still get genuine rounding practice; they just won't see ambiguous banker's-rounding edge cases.
Diff 2 — Escape Lines bank, T10 mk() function
Location: inside banks['Escape Lines'] array, 4th entry (Round {a} to nearest {b}). Search for the string: Math.round(a/b)*b).toFixed(2)

gen(): change (Math.random()*5).toFixed(3) to (Math.random()*5).toFixed(2)  — restricts to 2dp values, removing the 3dp .xx5 boundary class. D3 authored questions (M27p6: 0.994→0.01, M29v2: 7.445→0.01) are authored data, not generated, so they are unaffected.

Step 2 — Training Gallery T10 generator. Find exact string:
Step 3 — Escape Lines T10 generator. Find exact string:
Verification checklist (run in browser console after patch)
roundTo(246,10) — should still use the b>=1 integer path → 250 (no change to integer rounding branch)
Run 100 Training Gallery T10 prompts in game — confirm no prompt shows a float artifact in the answer or hint text
Run 50 Escape Lines T10 prompts — confirm same
Add a single Heist Briefing screen before the first puzzle with (1) objective statement, (2) controls + costs summary, (3) one worked example of answering a math lock.
No statement of the overall goal — the player does not know they are cracking a vault or what winning looks like
No statement that prompts are math questions — the terminal just appears with no framing
No tutorial or worked example before the first live prompt
'Heat' used before it is defined — 'Esc to cancel (costs heat)' has no explanation of what heat is or its maximum
'H for hint (−50)' — −50 of what is never stated

Add a Heist Briefing screen. Text: 'You're cracking the Echo Vault. Each lock is a math puzzle. Type your answer and press Enter. Crack all N locks before heat reaches 100 to escape.' Include one worked example. Define heat, score, and hint cost explicitly.
No lock counter visible — player cannot tell how many locks remain or what completing them achieves
No heat threshold labeled — player cannot see 'Heat: 40/100' so they don't know how close to failure they are
Win condition changes (vault → escape) with no announced objective change
HUD must show 'Locks: X/N', 'Heat: Y/100', 'Score: Z' at all times. Add a pre-play objective panel: '• Crack all N locks. • Keep heat below 100. • Escape once all locks are cracked.'
D3 — Stage Transition Clarity · PARTIAL / OPAQUE
Vault entry: no labeled overlay — player drops directly into the first terminal prompt with no stage marker
Escape phase: silent transition — guards accelerate, escape gate appears, no announcement
Class ability activation: no explanation of keys, cooldowns, or costs on first encounter
Focus mode (Tab): slows time and drains points — neither effect is explained before first use

Interrupting overlay (require keypress) for each new phase: vault entry, escape phase, first class ability use, first focus mode use. Each overlay names the phase and states the new rule in one sentence.
Answer format unstated: no instruction on whether to type integers, decimals, fractions, or percents
Cost of a wrong submitted answer is never stated — player has no way to know whether pressing Enter on an uncertain answer is risky
'Esc to cancel' — scope of cancel is ambiguous: current input, current lock, or whole mission
Hint behavior unstated: how many hints per lock, what form the hint takes

Briefing screen must include: 'Type as a number (e.g. 12, 3.5, −2). Fractions: 1/2. Percents: type the number only (e.g. 25 for 25%). Wrong answer: +10 Heat. Esc: skip this lock (+20 Heat). H: one hint per lock (−50 score).'
Wrong answer: no visible message showing what the correct answer was
Heat and score deltas are not shown as labeled numbers — bar moves but player cannot tell by how much
Esc and H actions produce no visible confirmation message

On correct: green 'LOCK CRACKED! +100 score'. On wrong: red 'Wrong. Answer: [X]. +10 Heat'. On hint: amber 'Hint shown. −50 score'. On Esc: orange 'Lock skipped. +20 Heat'. All as brief floating text overlays with labeled delta.
'Heat' used without definition — could mean temperature or fire to a Gr 6–8 student unfamiliar with stealth game conventions
'Cancel' is too broad — does not specify scope (input, lock, or mission)

Define in briefing: 'Heat = guard suspicion (0–100). If it hits 100 you're caught.' Replace 'Esc to cancel' with 'Esc = skip this lock (+20 Heat)'. Replace 'H for hint (−50)' with 'H = hint (costs 50 score)'.
Heat pressure from second one with no defined maximum or consequence — player fears submitting any answer
All three action costs (wrong answer, Esc, H) are ambiguous — player cannot make informed decisions
Sudden vault → escape transition with accelerating guards and no overlay explaining the new phase
Focus mode Tab cost (2pts/sec drain) is not explained before activation — player may hesitate to use a core scaffold tool
Esc is dual-purpose (skip lock + possible exit intent) — players afraid to accidentally skip a lock when trying to pause
🛠 Player Clarity Fix Tasks — Prototype Engineer
Show before Mission 1 and any mission with no prior play — requires keypress (Space or Enter) to dismiss
Section 1 — Objective: 'You're cracking the Echo Vault. Each lock is a math puzzle. Crack all locks before heat reaches 100 to escape.'
Section 2 — Costs & Controls: Wrong answer: +10 Heat | Esc = skip this lock (+20 Heat) | H = hint (−50 score, 1 per lock) | Tab = Focus (slows time, costs 2 pts/sec) | P = pause
Section 3 — Worked example: 'Lock: 3x + 5 = 14. You type: 3. Press Enter → LOCK CRACKED. +100 score.'
Section 4 — Answer formats: 'Type as a number (12, 3.5, −2). Fractions: 1/2. Percents: type digits only (25 for 25%).' If game accepts both 0.5 and 1/2, say so explicitly.
🔴 PC2 — Labeled Feedback on Every Action
Correct: green floating text 'LOCK CRACKED! +100 score' — 1200ms, top-center
Wrong: red floating text 'Wrong — answer was [X]. +10 Heat.' — 2000ms, shows correct answer always

Add lock counter to HUD: 'Locks: 2/5' visible at all times during mission
HUD score label: 'Score: 450' — not a raw number, needs the label

On Tab press for first time ever: show 1500ms non-blocking overlay 'FOCUS MODE — Time slows to 25% speed. Costs 2 points per second. Press Tab again to exit.' — use focusModeTipFired flag (localStorage)
While Focus is active: show purple banner 'FOCUS ACTIVE — 2 pts/sec' — same style as the existing softstep indicator
🟡 PC5 — Separate Pause Key from Esc
Assign P key to pause/settings (no heat cost). Esc remains 'skip this lock (+20 Heat)' only.
Update all control legend text to distinguish Esc (skip, costs heat) from P (pause, free)

All authored D3 rounding questions (M21p4, M23p3, M25p5, M27p6, M29p3, M29v2, M23v3) — these are static data and unchanged
